name: EmbedUpload
summary: Candidate mirror-uploader service pending first-party verification for upload-once fan-out across multiple downstream hosts.

account.benefits: Account requirements and feature differences are still pending first-party review.

developer.notes: Developer and automation support have not yet been verified from first-party sources.

allowed_file_types.notes: Allowed file types probably depend on downstream hosts, but that still needs first-party verification.

security.notes: The user-provided seed link was HTTP-only, and current HTTPS behavior still needs verification.

sources.label: EmbedUpload homepage test
sources.notes: Live browser test reached a Sedo domain-parking page instead of an active mirror-uploader product.

max_file_size.notes: Detailed mirror-upload limits for this candidate have not yet been verified from first-party sources.

guest_uploads.notes: Account requirements and feature differences are still pending first-party review.

remote_import.notes: Current first-party sources for this candidate have not yet verified a remote URL import workflow.

torrent_import.notes: Current first-party sources for this candidate have not yet verified a torrent import workflow.

stores_files_itself.notes: Standalone storage behavior for this mirror-uploader candidate has not yet been verified.

retention_model.notes: Retention likely depends on downstream mirrored hosts, but that still needs first-party verification.

downstream_dependency.notes: Bandwidth behavior has not yet been verified from first-party sources.

reason: Rejected after live browser test on 2026-05-01: the official domain resolves to a Sedo parked page, not a usable mirror-uploader service.

name: Mirrorace
summary: Mirror-uploader service for uploading a file once and fanning it out to multiple downstream file hosts.

account.benefits: Guest uploads are available. Accounts add file management, own-host credentials, API credentials, and other management features.

developer.notes: MirrorAce publishes official API documentation for upload, remote upload, file info, and account-authenticated requests.

allowed_file_types.notes: Allowed file types probably depend on downstream hosts, but that still needs first-party verification.

security.notes: The seed entry uses an HTTPS homepage, but deeper security claims still need first-party verification.

sources.label: MirrorAce homepage
sources.notes: Official homepage verified in a live browser. The page currently says uploading has been disabled, so the service is not publishable as usable today.

sources.label: MirrorAce FAQ
sources.notes: Official FAQ covering 5 GB upload size, guest uploads, non-hosting behavior, and retention of mirrored links.

sources.label: MirrorAce account types
sources.notes: Official account-types page showing 10 GB file size for no-account and free-account uploads.

sources.label: MirrorAce API documentation
sources.notes: Official API docs for upload, remote upload, file info, authenticated API credentials, and a current example response showing a 10 GB max_file_size.

max_file_size.notes: Current official account-types and API surfaces show 10 GB limits for active no-account and free-account uploads. The FAQ still mentions 5 GB, so older help text appears stale.

guest_uploads.notes: Guest uploads are available. Accounts add file management, own-host credentials, API credentials, and other management features.

remote_import.notes: MirrorAce publishes remote URL upload workflows on the homepage and in the API docs.

torrent_import.notes: Current first-party pages reviewed here do not clearly publish a torrent import workflow.

stores_files_itself.notes: MirrorAce says it is not a file-hosting site and keeps mirror links rather than hosting the mirrored files itself.

retention_model.notes: Conditional: MirrorAce says links remain in its database unless they violate terms, while the underlying mirrored files ultimately depend on downstream hosts.

downstream_dependency.notes: Mirrored file availability and download behavior ultimately depend on the selected downstream hosts.

reason: Rejected after live browser test on 2026-05-01: the official homepage currently says uploading has been disabled, so it is not meaningfully usable today.

name: Mirrored
summary: Mirror-uploader service that uploads files to multiple downstream hosts and returns an aggregate download page.

account.benefits: Guest uploads are supported for hosts that allow anonymous uploads. Accounts add file management and the ability to upload into the user's own accounts on supported hosts.

developer.notes: Mirrored publishes official HTTPS API documentation for uploads, account info, and file information.

allowed_file_types.notes: Allowed file types depend on selected downstream hosts; the current homepage lists regular file hosts and a separate group of video-only hosts.

security.notes: The live product and API documentation are served over HTTPS. Mirrored does not position itself as end-to-end encrypted storage.

sources.label: Mirrored homepage
sources.notes: Official homepage verified in a live browser: active local-file and remote-URL upload forms, 750 MB regular upload limit, multiple host choices, and current free service messaging.

sources.label: Mirrored FAQ
sources.notes: Official FAQ covering anonymous uploads, non-hosting behavior, and mirror-service workflow.

sources.label: Mirrored API docs
sources.notes: Official API documentation for authenticated HTTPS uploads and file/account info.

max_file_size.notes: The current official homepage says regular users can upload up to 750 MB per file, while PRO users can upload up to 3 GB per file.

guest_uploads.notes: Guest uploads are supported for hosts that allow anonymous uploads. Accounts add file management and the ability to upload into the user's own accounts on supported hosts.

remote_import.notes: The current official upload page exposes a Remote URL Upload mode.

torrent_import.notes: Current first-party pages expose local-file upload and remote URL upload modes, but do not publish a torrent or magnet import workflow.

stores_files_itself.notes: Official FAQ says Mirrored is not a file-hosting site; mirrored files live on downstream hosts and Mirrored keeps aggregate links.

retention_model.notes: Conditional: Mirrored stores aggregate links and background-mirrors files to downstream hosts, so long-term availability depends on both Mirrored's link database and the selected mirror hosts.

downstream_dependency.notes: Mirrored availability and download behavior depend on the selected downstream hosts and their own rules.

name: Z-o-o-m
summary: Freeware Windows File & Image Uploader desktop app that uploads local files/folders to many downstream file and image hosts, supports host account settings, drag-and-drop queues, scheduler, speed limiter, proxy support, and Wine on Linux.

account.benefits: The program is freeware for common users; downstream host accounts can be configured per host, and a registered/donor version unlocks extra benefits.

developer.notes: Z-o-o-m is a GUI desktop uploader. The cited first-party pages do not publish a public API or command-line workflow for automating Z-o-o-m itself.

allowed_file_types.notes: Allowed file types depend on the selected downstream host. The program is positioned for both file and image uploads and supports choosing multiple hosts.

public_sharing.notes: The app uploads to downstream hosts and returns/copies links; the help page documents link/history tabs and link-copy formatting.

security.notes: The website is HTTPS. The privacy policy says account cookies/tokens can be stored locally and protected with AES-256 when the user sets a password, but downstream host storage security depends on each selected service.

sources.label: Z-o-o-m homepage
sources.notes: Official homepage verifies File & Image Uploader positioning, newest version 8.5.9 updated 2026-04-25, supported-host update history, and ongoing host/account support.

sources.label: Z-o-o-m download page
sources.notes: Official download page verifies the Windows executable, speed limiter, SOCKS/HTTP proxy support, adapter/IP binding, and Wine compatibility on Linux.

sources.label: Z-o-o-m help page
sources.notes: Official help page verifies upload queues, adding files/folders, drag-and-drop, starting/stopping/re-uploading, selecting one or multiple hosts, host account settings, scheduler, and link-copy formatting.

sources.label: Z-o-o-m FAQ
sources.notes: Official FAQ verifies freeware status for common users, 10 GB/day freeware upload cap, up to 8 simultaneous files, registered-version benefits, and support/reporting workflow.

sources.label: Z-o-o-m privacy policy
sources.notes: Official privacy policy verifies anonymous website/software use, local storage of downstream host login cookies/tokens, optional AES-256 protection for saved settings, and Google API scopes for YouTube, Google Photos, and Google Drive uploads.

max_file_size.notes: Z-o-o-m does not publish one universal per-file cap because selected downstream hosts determine file-size limits. The FAQ instead publishes a 10 GB/day freeware upload cap and up to 8 simultaneous files.

guest_uploads.notes: The program is freeware for common users and can upload through supported host modules; downstream services may still require accounts for specific hosts or features.

remote_import.notes: The cited help and download pages describe local file/folder upload queues and host account settings, but do not publish a remote URL import workflow.

torrent_import.notes: The cited first-party pages do not publish a torrent or magnet import workflow.

stores_files_itself.notes: Z-o-o-m is uploader software; files are stored by the selected downstream hosts, not by Z-o-o-m as a storage provider.

retention_model.notes: Retention depends on the selected downstream file/image host and any account used there; Z-o-o-m itself stores links/history/settings locally rather than hosting the files.

downstream_dependency.value: Selected downstream hosts determine upload, retention, and download behavior
downstream_dependency.notes: The uploader's behavior depends on the selected host modules, downstream host limits, and configured account credentials.
